titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 578

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=898 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:12:05.565452+00 | translation moa=899 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:12:05.575631+00

p.cl: M . Rex Scot’ . Omnibus probis hominibus tocius terre sue . salutem .

precipio ubicumque prior
de goldingaham . aut clientes sui  fugitiuos iuste ad goldingaham pertinentes inue
nire poterunt quatinus illos iuste habeant sine disturbacione . et vexatione . et prohibeo
ne aliquis uestrum eos iniuste detineat super meam defensionem .

Testibus Waltero Cancellario
Hugone de Morevilla .
Malcolm, king of the Scots, to all worthy men of his entire land, greeting.

I instruct that anywhere the prior of Coldingham or his servants shall be able to find fugitives legitimately belonging to Coldingham, they may lawfully have them without disturbance and harrassment; and I forbid that anyone of you unlawfully withhold them, on pain of my prohibition.
As witnesses: Walter the chancellor, Hugh de Morville.
